steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic noun
Adding ال makes it specific and adds shadda to the ش
Added و meaning 'and' at the start, and changed the ending to have a kasra (i) sound because it's being sworn by

steps.m: to shine brightly in the morning

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to shine' into noun meaning 'morning light'
Adding the feminine singular pronoun suffix ها to indicate possession/belonging
Added و at the beginning to mean 'and'
Connected the letters with fatha vowels to create the basic noun for the moon
Added ال to make it specific and ِ at the end because it's being sworn by

steps.m: and the moon (object of oath, followed by إِذَا تَلَىٰهَا - when it follows it)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect it with what comes before. This word starts an oath, with إِذَا تَلَىٰهَا (when it follows it) describing when.
This is the basic form of the word that means a specific point in time
Adding ا at the end makes it mean 'when' and connects it to what happens next. Here it introduces what happens when the moon follows.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
The alif is written as a dotless ya with an alif mark above it when something is attached after it
Added ها which means 'it' referring to the moon (القمر) mentioned in the ayah
Root letters connected with a-vowels (fatha) to make a noun meaning 'bright flow'
Added alif to make it a noun meaning the time when light flows
Added 'al' (the) at the start and changed the ending to 'i' because it comes after 'wa' (and) in an oath

steps.m: and the daytime (sworn by in an oath)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to mean 'and'. In this ayah, it's used to make an oath, like saying 'By the daytime!'

steps.m: when (introduces what happens in النَّهَارِ - the day)
steps.n: This is a word that means 'when' and is used to introduce something that definitely happens or will happen. Here it introduces what happens during the day (النَّهَارِ). The action it introduces is جَلَّىٰهَا (it brightens it).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Added shadda on lām to show intensity, and the weak letter و changed to ى
هَا means 'it/her' - refers back to something feminine that was mentioned
The هَا was already there - the only change is spelling جَلَّىٰ with an alif instead of alif maqsurah (ى), but means the same thing
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a proper noun
Added kasra at the end to show this word belongs to/is connected to something

steps.m: and the night (followed by إِذَا يَغْشَىٰهَا - when it covers it)
steps.n: Starts with 'wa' meaning 'and', followed by 'the night' - connects to next phrase about covering
This is the basic form of the word that means a specific point in time

steps.m: when (referring to يَغْشَىٰهَا - it covers it)
steps.n: Adding ا at the end makes it refer to future or conditional time. In this ayah, it introduces what happens when the night covers something.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The و changes to ا at the end because it's a weak letter.
Adding ي at the start changes it from past to present tense

steps.m: he covers/is covering it (referring to the night covering the earth)
steps.n: Added هَا at the end to show what is being covered. In this ayah, it refers to the earth being covered by the night.
Changed from root letters to past tense verb meaning 'it rose up'
Changed from a verb meaning 'to rise' into a noun meaning 'sky' by adding ء at the end
Added ال to make it specific, and doubled the س with shadda
Added و at the beginning to mean 'and'

steps.m: and by the sky (as an oath)
steps.n: The و changed from meaning 'and' to meaning 'by' (for making an oath), and the word got a special ending ِ because it's being used in an oath
This is a particle word that means 'what' - it doesn't come from a root
Adding و at the beginning means 'and'
Added an alif maksura (ىٰ) with a small alif above it to show the proper way to write this word when it ends with ى
Added the feminine object pronoun هَا meaning 'it/her' to show what was built (referring back to السَّمَآءِ 'the sky' which is feminine)
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a proper noun
Added ال to make it specific - we're talking about THE earth
Added وَ (and) at the start and ِ at the end because it's connected to something else in the sentence
This is a particle word that can mean 'what' or act as a relative pronoun
Added 'وَ' meaning 'and'. This word connects to طَحَىٰهَا (spread it out) in the ayah, asking about Allah's creation of the earth.
Added fathas to show this happened in the past. The و at the end changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end of a past tense verb.
The alif changes to a special form of ى (called alif maqsurah) with a small alif above it (ىٰ) when it appears before attached pronouns.

steps.m: He spread it (the earth) out flat
steps.n: Added هَا which refers back to الأرض (the earth) mentioned earlier in the verse. This shows what was spread out.
The root letters combine to form the noun 'soul' or 'self'. This is what's being sworn by in this ayah.
Added و at the beginning to mean 'and', connecting this oath with previous ones

steps.m: and by the soul (being sworn by)
steps.n: The tanween (two kasras) at the end shows this is part of an oath, like saying 'I swear by...'
This is a particle word that connects ideas and can mean 'what' or 'that which'
Added و at the beginning to mean 'and'. This word is connected to سَوَّىٰهَا (perfected it) as its object - it's what was perfected.

steps.m: to make something balanced or complete

steps.n: Root letters س-و-ي got vowels (َ) to make 'he made balanced' in past tense
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger, showing the action was done thoroughly
Added هَا to show what was being perfected (referring back to نَفْس mentioned earlier)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding أ at the start makes the verb cause someone to do something
Adding ها shows who receives the inspiration. This word connects to فُجُورَهَا وَتَقْوَىٰهَا which tells what was inspired: her evil and her good

steps.m: and then He inspired her (فُجُورَهَا وَتَقْوَىٰهَا)
steps.n: Adding ف means 'and then' or 'so'

steps.m: to break out/burst forth in disobedience

steps.n: Changed the action into a noun by adding و between letters and ُ marks, making it mean the state or result of breaking out in disobedience
Added هَا to show it belongs to her, and َ because it's receiving the action of inspiration in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'protected' to 'protection' and added ها ('her/its') at the end to show whose protection. This is what was inspired in the soul/self.
Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous word فُجُورَهَا (its evil), making the pair 'its evil and its protection'
This is a helper word that makes the verb أَفْلَحَ (succeeded) stronger - like saying 'definitely succeeded' instead of just 'succeeded'

steps.m: to succeed, prosper, achieve what is desired

steps.n: Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Adding أ at the beginning changes it from 'succeed' to 'make succeed'
This is a special word that points to people - like saying 'whoever' or 'the one who' in English. In this ayah, it refers to the person who purifies something.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added shadda to show stronger action. The و changes to ى because it's at the end
Added alif maksura mark to show the long vowel sound

steps.m: he purified it/her (object of أَفْلَحَ - succeeded)
steps.n: Added هَا to show what was purified. This word is connected to أَفْلَحَ (succeeded) in the ayah.
This is a particle that adds certainty to past actions
Added و at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it, like saying "and" in English

steps.m: to fail or lose out completely
steps.n: This is the basic meaning of the root
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
The middle letter changes to alif which is common for hollow verbs
This is a relative pronoun that refers to the person who does the action in دَسَّىٰهَا (corrupts it). In this ayah, it refers to the person who corrupts their soul.
This is the basic meaning of the root
Connected the root letters with fathas to make a past tense verb
Two changes happen: 1) The middle letter gets doubled with shadda to show the action is done thoroughly, 2) The و changes to ى at the end because it's a weak letter
Added هَا at the end to show what was buried. This هَا means 'it' or 'her' and is what receives the action of burying.
Doubling of middle root letter shows this is a strong or repeated denial. This word connects to ثَمُودُ (Thamud) who are doing the denying.
The ت at the end shows this is about ثَمُودُ (treated as feminine) doing the action in the past

steps.m: Thamud (name of an ancient Arabian tribe)
steps.n: This is a proper noun (name) that doesn't come from a root. It refers to an ancient Arabian tribe mentioned in the Quran.
The ُ (damma) at the end shows this is the subject doing the action in كَذَّبَتْ (she denied/rejected)
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a past tense verb
The verb 'he crossed limits' changes to a noun 'act of crossing limits' by adding ى at the end
Added ها ('its/her') at the end to show who did the bad action
Adds preposition ب meaning 'by/with'
This is the basic form of the word that shows when something happened in the past
The kasra at the end connects this word to what follows it, showing that 'when' is linked to the action 'arose/went forth'
This is the basic meaning of sending or dispatching something
The ان shows this happened by itself or to the subject
The small م (ۢ) shows that the ن blends into the ب sound when reading. This happens after the word إِذِ (when) in this ayah. The word means 'rose up quickly' or 'sprang forth'.
This root relates to misery and wretchedness
Changed from root letters to past tense verb by adding vowels - means someone became unhappy/miserable
Added أ at the beginning to show this is the most or worst form of being wretched
Added the long alif mark (ٰ) because this word ends with a weak letter

steps.m: their most wretched one (doer of انبعث)
steps.n: Added ها at the end to show this refers to their most wretched person. This person is the one who did the action of انبعث (went forth).
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter). This is a common pattern in Arabic for past tense verbs with و in the middle.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened after something else. This word is connected to لَهُمْ (to them) in the ayah.
Added fatha to show the basic vowel of the preposition

steps.m: to/for them (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who the preposition is directed to. This word connects to رَسُولُ ٱللَّهِ (the Messenger of Allah) who is speaking to them.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changes from a verb (sent) to a noun (messenger) by adding و
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject (doer) of the verb فَقَالَ (he said) in this ayah
Changed to the unique name for the One True God by adding ال and merging letters
Added kasra at the end to show possession, shadda for emphasis, and changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word. This shows the she-camel belongs to Allah.
Forms the noun for a female camel by adding alif and taa marboota
The fatha at the end shows this word is the object of a verb
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'

This is the unique name for the one true God in Islam. The ال makes it definite and the shadda emphasizes the L. The kasra at the end shows it belongs to or connects with something else in the sentence.

steps.m: to give drink/water to someone or something

steps.n: Adding fathas makes this a past tense verb meaning 'he watered/gave drink'
Changes from a verb (gave drink) to a noun meaning the action of giving drink

steps.m: her drinking (referring to the she-camel's drinking mentioned in the ayah)
steps.n: Added ها which means 'her' to show it's the she-camel's drinking. The alif changes to ى (called alif maqsurah) before adding ها

steps.m: and her drinking (connected to the previous phrase about the she-camel)
steps.n: Added conjunction وَ (and) to connect to previous phrase
The doubled middle letter shows doing this action with intensity or repeatedly
Added وا to show a group of people (they) did this action in the past

steps.m: they strongly rejected him as false
steps.n: Added هُ to show who they rejected (him)

steps.m: and so they strongly rejected him as false
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happened as a result of what came before
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Transforms singular past tense to plural past tense

steps.m: they cut/severed her (referring to the she-camel)
steps.n: Adds feminine singular object pronoun to show the she-camel being cut/severed

steps.m: so they cut/severed her (referring to the she-camel) (after فَكَذَّبُوهُ - they denied him)
steps.n: Adds coordinating conjunction 'so/then' to connect with previous statement
Added vowels to make it past tense. The repeated دم-دم pattern shows the action happened over and over.
Added فَ to show this happened as a result of the previous action (them rejecting and killing). This connects it to فَكَذَّبُوهُ (so they denied him) in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is above or over something else. It doesn't come from a root.
The ى changes to يْ to prepare for adding a pronoun. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
Added هِمْ at the end to show this applies to 'them' - referring to the people who denied the messenger in this ayah.

steps.m: to care for and help grow/develop
one who cares for and helps others grow
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this person does a lot of caring and helping

steps.m: the one who cares for and helps others grow
steps.n: Adding the ُ mark shows this is the main subject being talked about

steps.m: their Lord (the one who cares for and helps them grow)
steps.n: Adding هُم shows this Lord belongs to them or is connected to them

steps.m: to commit a wrong or mistake

steps.n: Changes from action to the result of that action. This wrong deed is what led to their Lord's punishment mentioned in the ayah.
Shows this wrong belonged to them (the people of Thamud mentioned earlier)

steps.m: because of their wrong (which led to punishment from رَبُّهُم)
steps.n: The بِ shows this wrong was the reason for their punishment. It connects to فَدَمْدَمَ (so He destroyed) in the ayah.
Added the alif at the end (written as ىٰ) because the word ends with a weak letter

steps.m: he leveled it (referring to the earth/city)
steps.n: Added هَا at the end to show what was leveled

steps.m: and so he leveled it (after they killed the camel)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened as a result of them killing the camel mentioned before
This is a negative particle that negates the verb يَخَافُ (fears) that comes after it, changing its meaning to 'does not fear'
The و connects this negation to what came before it, while لا negates the verb يَخَافُ (fears) that follows, making the combined meaning 'and does not fear'
This is the basic meaning of the root
The middle weak letter و changes to ا in past tense
Added يَ at the beginning to show it's happening now. Changed the last vowel to ُ because it's present tense.
This is the basic meaning of the root
Changed the root into a noun by adding vowel marks to mean 'the result' or 'what comes after'
Added ٰ at the end to show this is the ultimate or final result

steps.m: its final outcome (referring to the punishment mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added هَا to show this is talking about the outcome of something specific mentioned before
